notes: Output is {"added": ids} with ids=make([]string,len(tasks)) so it serializes [] never null. Exit codes: ExitUsage(2) missing/invalid JSON (decoder detail routed to hint, never error msg L62-66), ExitFile(3) IO, ExitValidation(1) entry-rule violations each with a tailored hint (L214-223). --stdin/--bulk/--spec handled; --compact/--quiet/--json via output pkg.

Asserts the []-not-null contract on every task slice (depends_on/source_sections/tags, L240-244) and import parity (L260-262); exit-code+hint coverage: blank id/title/acceptance/anchor/unknown-dep exit 1 with hint naming the field (L46-126), invalid JSON exit 2 with decoder detail in hint not error (L128-155). Also guards lint structured_elements [] and stats tags [].

Asserts exit 4 payload carries suggested_files as a JSON array (ElementsMatch L102, [] when empty L120-122), survives --compact (L149-161), and the success-path files field (L166-178). Flag-conflict guard exits 2 with actionable messages (L199-213). Hints name --affected-files/--affected-from-tasks (L104-106).

auditResult: Files/Checklist/Prompts are non-omitempty and always make-init (buildChecklist L589, generateRoleAuditPrompts L272) -> [] not null; FileSummary *ptr omitempty, SkippedRoles *[]ptr omitempty normalized to [] when set (L251-253). --compact drops file_summary + skipped_roles + overlap, keeps suggested_files on the exit-4 path (exitAuditNoFiles L829-835). Exit codes 2/3/4 + hints throughout.

Summary shape {merged_count,input_files,duplicates_removed,by_status,by_role,findings,overlap_report?,output_path/rows}. rows=make([]...,0) L124 and unique=make L172 -> [] not null. by_status/by_role are maps (json.Marshal sorts keys -> deterministic). dedupAuditRows sorts by (role,item_id) L183-192. overlap_report gated by !IsCompact L85. Exit 2 (no files/spec-positional), 3 (missing file).

Asserts merged_count/duplicates_removed/findings counts and by_status breakdown (L36-43), exact unique-row count in -o file (L47-53), empty/all-malformed inputs exit 0 with merged_count 0 + zero-byte -o (L59-103), no-input exit 2 (L107-112), missing file exit 3 (L116-121), spec-positional rejected exit 2 with no parse side-effects (L128-142).

computeAuditOverlapReport preserves cluster insertion order via an order slice (L22-36) then delegates to engine.OverlapReport which returns make([]RoleOverlap,0) sorted by role (overlap.go L66-76) -> [] not null. latestAuditRoundOverlapReport returns explicit []engine.RoleOverlap{} on no-round/missing-file (L44-50, L48-50). PASS rows excluded from clustering.

Asserts the prompt-body contract: round 1 has no Prior Round section (L26-39); round 2+ carries only this role's non-PASS rows as JSON with changed_since flag, excludes PASS and other roles' rows (L67-83); a role with no prior non-PASS carries no section (L86-89); legacy rounds carry a positional/not-comparable disclaimer (L127-147).

Asserts fixed body section order Role<Rules<Excerpt<Checklist<Files<Schema (L35-44), checklist embedded as a JSON array identical to checklist_items with expected_evidence never empty (L46-55), and byte-identical deterministic regeneration across runs incl stable ids (L69-94). output_path set per prompt (audit-r%d-%s.ndjson).

Ignores exactly the local-state artifacts (local.json active pointer + CLI defaults, locks/ flock dir) while keeping .tp/config.json and role files committed. No JSON-emitting code here, so no shape/null/compact/exit-code surface to violate.

Single locked write path: addTasks wraps read-modify-write in engine.WithFileLock (L186); WriteTaskFile at L259 inside the lock. All errors routed through output.Error+os.Exit (L49,64,94,112,189,214,244,260). scanner.Err() checked at L121 with stderr warning. Paths from engine.DiscoverTaskFile (validated), no string-concat paths. No os.WriteFile in this file.

All file writes use 0o600 via require.NoError (L27,249,253,269). All paths via filepath.Join. No _=err patterns; errors checked through testify require/assert. Test helpers (readRawTaskFile, extractSlice) propagate errors correctly.

All writes 0o600 via require.NoError (L30,41,50,130-132). gitOut checks cmd.Output error via require.NoError (L23). All paths via filepath.Join. toStringSlice uses ok-pattern type assertion (not error swallow). No lock/write-path concerns (test code).

L214 auditWf, _ := engine.ResolveWorkflow(specPath, flagFile) discards the error with _. If the task file/workflow block is corrupt or unreadable, the audit silently proceeds with zero-value workflow fields (AuditCleanRounds=0, AuditMaxRounds=0), masking the failure in prompt framing (L221 passes those values to generateRoleAuditPrompts). The same ResolveWorkflow/LoadReviewState calls are properly error-checked elsewhere in this file (loadAuditSpec L308-321, loadAuditPriorRound L349-356 with IsMissingStateIndex), making the inconsistency notable. Fix: check the error and either exitStateError or fall back to engine.DefaultWorkflow() explicitly. Secondary note: L217 LoadReviewState checks err==nil but does not distinguish missing-index from corruption like L310 does.

loadAuditMergeRows opens files with os.Open (L126) and closes them with f.Close() at L161 without checking the returned error — a swallowed return per the go-safety rule. The Close is also not deferred, so any future os.Exit/panic added between L131 and L161 would leak the fd. Practical impact is low (read-only file, no os.Exit in the scanner loop body), but the idiomatic fix is defer f.Close() immediately after the error guard. All other error paths are correct: os.Stat existence check (L118), os.Open error→exit (L128), json.Unmarshal→warning+skip (L140), scanner.Err()→warning (L158). Output write uses 0o600 (L90). No lock needed (merge does not write task files).
All writes 0o600 via require.NoError (L21,25). Exit-code assertions via require.Equal (L32,60,75,103,111,121,142). All paths via filepath.Join. No swallowed errors; json.Unmarshal checked (L35).

latestAuditRoundOverlapReport properly handles parseNDJSONFile error at L52-53 (returns empty slice, documented behavior). computeAuditOverlapReport uses asString() helper for safe map[string]any type conversion (no ok-value swallow). filepath.Join for state-dir path (L51). Read-only: no writes, no locks needed. Empty slices explicitly initialized ([]engine.RoleOverlap{} at L45,49).

All writes 0o600 via require.NoError (L19-21,28-29,49-50). MkdirAll uses 0o755 (L19). All paths via filepath.Join. writeRecordedAuditRound and auditPriorChangedSince check all errors via require. Unchecked type assertions (p[prompt].(string) L121, L37) are acceptable in test assertion context.

All writes 0o600 via require.NoError (L15-18,71-72). All paths via filepath.Join. Exit codes asserted via require.Equal (L21,24). No swallowed errors; type assertions (byRole[role][prompt].(string) L28,71) acceptable in test context. findItemEvidence helper returns clean int.
Not Go source — two ignore patterns (local.json, locks/). No file writes, locks, error paths, or permissions apply; no go-safety concern.

var tasks []model.Task (l.100) never reaches JSON — addTasks guards len()==0 at l.163; all JSON-bound slices use make() (ids l.265, resolvable l.196, existing l.205); doc comments on decodeTaskJSON/computeAccuracyRatio/addTasks; no exported symbols undocumented; no TODO/FIXME; addTasks at 113 lines but cohesive locked-write with inline validation; gofmt clean.

got := make([]string, 0, len(tf.Tasks)) at l.177; all test helpers documented (initEntryProject, errJSON, readRawTaskFile, extractSlice); consistent testify patterns; no TODO/FIXME; no dead code; gofmt clean.

toStringSlice returns make([]string, 0, len(arr)) at l.70; doneTaskJSON builds make([]string, len(shas)) at l.59; all helpers documented (gitOut, commitFile, writeTaskFileRaw, newAuditRepo, closeTaskFile); consistent naming; no TODO/FIXME; gofmt clean.

Exported ChecklistItem documented (l.35-36); nil→[]engine.SkippedRole{} guard at l.251-254 prevents null-vs-[] bug; result.Files assigned from make()-initialized slices (filtered l.495); runAudit at 110 lines (handler tolerance); errors are os.Exit consumed not propagated; no TODO/FIXME; gofmt clean.

All three functions documented (runAuditMerge, loadAuditMergeRows, dedupAuditRows); rows := make([]map[string]any, 0) at l.124; unique := make([]map[string]any, 0, len(rows)) at l.172; byStatus/byRole maps properly initialized; runAuditMerge at 90 lines (within tolerance); errors consumed via os.Exit; no TODO/FIXME; gofmt clean.

All test cases documented with §-references (§3.3, §4.1); consistent runTP/runTPMerge helper usage; clean assertion patterns; no nil-slice concerns in test output; no TODO/FIXME; no dead code; gofmt clean.

Both functions documented; latestAuditRoundOverlapReport returns []engine.RoleOverlap{} empty literals (l.45, l.49) — correct nil avoidance; clusters/order/roleSets all make()-initialized; short focused functions (22 and 13 lines); no TODO/FIXME; gofmt clean.

Helper writeRecordedAuditRound documented; all test cases carry §10.2 references; auditPriorChangedSince helper well-structured; consistent testify usage; no nil-slice output concerns; no TODO/FIXME; no dead code; gofmt clean.

ids := make([]string, 0) at l.86 — proper make() for collected slice; findItemEvidence helper documented; deterministic-regeneration test well-structured; consistent assertion patterns; no TODO/FIXME; gofmt clean.
2-line gitignore (local.json, locks/); no Go code — role rules (error wrapping, doc comments, function length, nil-slice, TODO/FIXME, dead code) are not applicable.
tp brief and tp next --brief assemble the brief from durable state via engine.BuildBrief — the tool briefs the unit (P4).
Brief.Text() assembles a self-sufficient five-section brief carrying identity, scope, prior work, the unit, and the close recipe.
Brief struct has {identity, task, prior_work, close, scope} — the same structural shape every phase gets.
buildPriorWorkEntry carries the first line of closed_reason, commit_shas, and commit_files — what was produced, not a count.
ScopeFenceText is a statement in the brief (not a commit-path check); commit_files records drift without blocking (model/task.go SetCommitFiles).
BuildBrief assembles from existing durable state — the brief replaces re-typed orchestrator context, not adds new context.
doneTransitiveDeps walks depends_on closure; topoOrderSubset returns them in dependency-safe order — always included beyond the cap.
recencyCandidates collects done non-dep tasks; resolveRecencyLimit caps at 5 by default within the 12-entry total.
No .tp/brief.md or implementation-brief role corpus exists; only review/audit roles are customizable.
ScopeFenceText is explicitly a statement, not a commit-path check; no file-inference or commit-rejection logic exists.
BuildBrief is called on demand each invocation; no brief artifact is stored to disk.
tp brief returns text or JSON; it spawns no agent and resets no context.
Converged/ConsecutiveClean/phase-detection logic is unchanged from v0.29.0.
runBrief is read-only; selectBriefTarget never claims or writes. Brief.Text() outputs all five sections in order. Verified by brief_test.go.
BuildNextAction sets BriefCommand per phase: tp next --brief (implement), tp review --round N (review), tp audit (audit).
loadAuditPriorRound filters to non-PASS rows, groups by role; buildRolePrompt passes only priorByRole[role.ID]. Round-1 has no prior rounds (returns nil). Verified by audit_prior_round_test.go.
fileCheckItems builds file-<role>-<slug> ids from subject; slugifySubject is deterministic. dedupAuditRows (audit_merge.go:170-194) dedups on (role, item_id). Verified by audit_slug_test.go.

renderFraming states output_path, the stop-after-this-round rule, round number, required/current clean counts, and remaining when capped. Applied to both review (review.go:784) and audit (audit_roles.go:255) prompts.
File-reading statement covers three cases: no files (complete), inlined files (complete, no read needed), named paths only (read yourself). 12KB budget enforced in generateRoleAuditPrompts (L313).
countAuditFindings warns naming file and line on missing role; round still recorded. parseRecordRows (review_record.go:149-151) mirrors for review.

Switch ensures implicit tasks go to implicitDuration (not excluded); claimed from claim/next, implicit from done/commit. Covered-by exempt. Verified by duration_source_test.go (3 tests on disjoint/precedence).
compact omits spec_excerpt (L497-499); compactPriorWork drops file lists keeping id+summary; Brief.Text() keeps acceptance, close, scope under compact.
IsLegacyRound detects by missing id_scheme marker; renderPriorRoundSection (audit_roles.go:193-195) states positional incomparability. Verified by TestAuditPriorRound_LegacyRoundDisclaimer.
go test ./... passes (all packages), golangci-lint run reports 0 issues. SKILL.md, README.md, REFERENCE.md all carry brief/commit_files/duration_source/output_path/Out-of-scope docs.
selectBriefTarget emits {done, message} and exits ExitState (4) when no task is available or id is unknown (L65-68).
--brief + --minimal exits 2 (L43-47); outputNextBrief (L238-254) claims via the normal next path then returns the brief; --json composes.
SelectPriorWork lists transitive deps + recency to default cap; each entry carries evidence first line, shas, files capped at 5 (+N more); --prior 0 keeps deps, drops recency, states omitted count.
renderPriorWorkText returns 'This is the first unit...' when IsFirstUnit is true (deps and candidates both empty).

Single (L290-293), batch (L793-796), and tp commit (commit.go:146) all call SetCommitFiles. But runDoneMulti (L516-518) calls SetCommitSHAs only — SetCommitFiles is missing. A multi-ID close with --commit records commit_shas but not commit_files. covered-by/set-reject/reopen-clear all verified correct.
SetCommitFiles deduplicates, sorts, caps at maxCommitFiles=50, stores total in CommitFilesTotal. Verified by TestCommitFiles_CapFiftyWithTotal.
CloseRecipeText resolves builtin/hc recipes, names the gate, the -- separator, and the rejected-command list under hc. auto is resolved upstream by EffectiveCommitStrategy.
ExtractOutOfScope parses the trailing 'Out of scope:' line; report.go L140-145 surfaces it as out_of_scope field. Closed_reason preserves the line verbatim.
Implementation brief carries Identity (identityOneLine at L443, set as Brief.Identity at L502); the asymmetry described for the implementation unit is closed.
ScopeFenceText() provides the scope fence; set as Brief.Scope at L506; rendered in ## Scope section of Brief.Text().
BriefTask carries id, acceptance, source_sections, source_lines, spec_excerpt — matching the implementation-unit input pointer column.
SelectPriorWork assembles prior-work entries; Brief.PriorWork set at L504; rendered in ## Prior work section.
CloseRecipeText builds the output contract (close recipe + evidence contract); Brief.Close set at L505.
CloseRecipeText embeds the resolved quality_gate command verbatim and the red-gate-never-closed rule.
identityOneLine const states the one-unit-then-stop rule; rendered in ## Identity section at L535-546.
Loop budget stays 'not applicable' for implementation units — Brief struct has no loop-budget field, matching the design.
## Identity section in Brief.Text() states what the unit is and the one-unit-then-stop rule.
## Scope section carries the acceptance boundary plus ScopeFenceText prohibitions (§7).
## Prior work section rendered from SelectPriorWork output per §5.
## Your unit section carries task id, acceptance verbatim, anchors, spec_excerpt.
## How to close section carries the exact close recipe from CloseRecipeText for the effective commit_strategy.
closeRecipeCommand default case emits 'tp done <id> --auto-commit -- "<evidence>"' for builtin.
closeRecipeCommand HC case emits 'commit with hc, then tp done <id> --commit <sha>' plus the rejected-invocation note.
EffectiveCommitStrategy resolves auto to hc-on-PATH else builtin; the recipe emits the matching concrete recipe.

loadAuditPriorRound loads last audit round, filters non-PASS, groups by role, computes changed-since via filesChangedSince. renderPriorRoundSection states re-check-not-verdict, changed-since omitted when no file path (priorAuditRow.ChangedSince *bool omitempty). 4 tests.

CloseRecipeText resolves effective strategy (builtin/hc, auto resolved upstream), embeds quality_gate verbatim, red-gate rule, evidence contract (one line per criterion, English, first line = next summary), -- separator, and hc rejected invocations.

tp brief [id] read-only, five sections in order, exits 4 ({done,message}) on no task/unknown id. --json returns {identity,task,prior_work,close,scope}. --compact drops excerpt+file-lists, shortens identity/scope, keeps acceptance/close/scope.

SelectPriorWork: transitive deps always included, recency to default 12-cap, each entry carries id/title/evidence-first-line/shas/files-capped-5. --prior 0-20 validated (L157-162, exit 2). First-unit and omitted-count handled.

ScopeFenceText states acceptance-only, no refactor/rename/reformat, no hand-editing task file/.tp-review, report out-of-fence as Out of scope: line. ExtractOutOfScope preserves verbatim; report surfaces it.

resolveCommitFiles (commit.go:344-364) resolves added/modified/deleted/renamed-new paths; SetCommitFiles (model/task.go:127-150) dedup/sort/cap-50. tp commit, done --auto-commit, done --commit (single), done --batch all call it. GAP: runDoneMulti (done.go:516-518) calls SetCommitSHAs but omits SetCommitFiles — multi-ID done with --commit records commit_shas without commit_files. covered-by/set-reject/reopen/git-unavailable all correct.

SKILL.md (L161: brief/scope-fence/next --brief/injection-duty), README.md (L87-96,312,376,402,420,512,635), REFERENCE.md (L28-37,150,190,209,211,264) all document brief/next --brief/--prior/brief_command/commit_files/duration_source/output_path/Out-of-scope. go test + golangci-lint clean.

claim sets DurationSourceClaimed (claim.go:125, next.go:172); implicit from done (done.go:203) and commit (commit.go:83); covered-by exempt. Report excludes implicit under implicit_duration, disjoint from excluded_from_accuracy. 11 tests in duration_source_test.go.
recordAuditRoundEntry stores IDScheme=IDSchemeSlug ('slug') on every audit round; IsLegacyRound (reviewstate.go:290-292) returns true when marker is empty. tp never rewrites recorded files.
outputNextBrief claims via the normal next path then builds the brief; --json composes (L249-250); --brief + --minimal exits 2 (L43-47). Verified by next_brief_test.go.

renderFraming carries output_path, reset discipline, loop budget (round/required/current/remaining), file-completeness statement. 12KB perRoleReadingBudget inlined for first under-budget role (audit_roles.go:313, review.go:776).
countAuditFindings warns 'line N of FILE missing role field' (L131-133) and still records. parseRecordRows mirrors for review (review_record.go:149-151).
BuildNextAction sets BriefCommand: tp next --brief (implement), tp review --round N (review), tp audit (audit). Decompose/release carry null. Verified by resume_brief_command_test.go.

fileCheckItems builds file-<role>-<slug>; slugifySubject lowercases, collapses non-alphanumeric to dash, trims, caps at 40, collision suffix -2/-3. dedupAuditRows dedups on (role, item_id). 5 tests in audit_slug_test.go.
